unit.note: client vitest 206 files / 2017 passed (2016 baseline + 1 new CI fence in ModalShell.test.tsx). server vitest 178 files / 2085 passed + 1 skipped (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local; untouched by this diff). tsc --noEmit clean; oxlint clean on the touched files. The new fence was falsified both ways: it PASSES with [&>*]:shrink-0 present and FAILS when the class is removed - it is a real guard, not decoration.

integration.note: No integration surface: the diff is a single CSS rule on a presentational shell plus two comments. No route, store, contract, or cross-component machinery touched.

e2e.note: F0.5 gate run (3 specs): the NEW triage-fix-now-more-options-clip.spec.ts + the 2 existing triage-fix-now.spec.ts cases (which exercise BOTH the new-task and new-iterate modal bodies). The new spec was confirmed RED before the fix (422px of the More-options panel clipped, modal body NOT scrollable) and GREEN after (0px clipped, body scrolls). Additionally re-run outside the gate: 80-tablet-responsive.spec.ts 21/21 passed (layout non-regression across the compact/desktop bands). 70-b-dropdown-modes + 50-new-task-modal fail/skip in the isolated stack, but fail IDENTICALLY on the untouched baseline (verified by stashing the fix and re-running): they need a project-SCOPED board (the app renders ProjectCreateCascade, not create-menu-primary, in All-projects scope). Pre-existing fixture gap, not a regression.

design_fidelity.note: No visual redesign - the fix RESTORES the intended rendering of an existing panel. Verified by real-browser screenshot before/after.

smoke.note: Isolated single-process stack (built client + SHIPWRIGHT_STATIC_DIR + tsx server on :3901, temp USERPROFILE, 127.0.0.1): /api/health 200, index served 200.

test_completeness.enumeration_basis: Every behavior this diff introduces or changes. The diff is one CSS rule on ModalShell's scroll body (plus two explanatory comments and one new data-testid), so the behavior set is: the rule's own presence (CI fence), the layout outcome it produces (5 distinct assertions in the new E2E), and non-regression of the surfaces the rule newly applies to (the other create-dialog bodies + the responsive bands) = 8 behaviors.

behaviors.behavior: ModalShell's scroll body pins [&>*]:shrink-0 (+ keeps overflow-y-auto and its max-h) so no direct child can absorb the container's overflow
behaviors.evidence: ModalShell.test.tsx 'modal body pins [&>*]:shrink-0...' - vitest is the CI gate here; Playwright is not. Falsified: removing the class turns this test RED.

behaviors.behavior: Opened from a triage item, the More-options panel renders UNCLIPPED (scrollHeight - clientHeight <= 1)
behaviors.evidence: triage-fix-now-more-options-clip.spec.ts - RED before fix (422px clipped), GREEN after (0px).

behaviors.behavior: The last field in the panel (Tags) sits fully inside the panel's painted box - the row the user saw sliced in half
The modal BODY takes the overflow and becomes scrollable (the second half of the bug: the disclosure ate the negative space, so the clipped fields were unreachable, not merely off-screen)
behaviors.evidence: triage-fix-now-more-options-clip.spec.ts scrollHeight > clientHeight on new-issue-modal-body. Doubles as the anti-vacuity guard: if the form ever fit, the clip assertions could pass trivially - this one fails loud instead.

behaviors.behavior: The clipped fields are actually reachable by scrolling
The triage auto-expand is PRESERVED (More options opens with aria-expanded=true so the carried-over priority/domain are visible) - the behavior the user explicitly chose to keep
The rule does not regress the other create-dialog bodies it now also applies to (new-task, new-iterate)
behaviors.evidence: triage-fix-now.spec.ts (2 cases: github->new-task, iterate->new-iterate) GREEN. Direct children of the scroll body were additionally enumerated across all five bodies in code review: none declares flex-1/shrink/basis, so shrink-0 is a no-op for every child except the overflow-hidden one it targets.

behaviors.behavior: No layout regression across the compact/desktop responsive bands
